elements.title: seaweed roofs
elements.contents: ... that the seaweed roofs (example pictured) of houses on the Danish island of Læsø were traditionally constructed by women?

elements.title: Norah Davis
elements.contents: ... that novelist Norah Davis once recommended putting a boy in a hypnotic trance to treat his amnesia?

elements.title: Tremate, empi, tremate
elements.contents: ... that Beethoven's "Tremate, empi, tremate" was not performed for ten years after it was written?

elements.title: Tom Beck
elements.contents: ... that, at the time he signed with the Denver Broncos, Tom Beck lived only a few houses away from the team's practice facility?

elements.title: Bunka Apartments
elements.contents: ... that the Bunka Apartments were intended to introduce the US middle-class lifestyle to Japan, but were so expensive that an average salaryman would have spent his entire monthly wage on rent alone?

elements.title: Brenda Meeks
elements.contents: ... that Regina Hall improvised parts of her role as Brenda Meeks in Scary Movie despite the director initially telling her not to "bother" the character and to follow the script?

elements.title: Adán Gordón
elements.contents: ... that Olympic swimmer Adán Gordón was called the "Human Fish"?

elements.title: Tulasi Srinivas
elements.contents: ... that Tulasi Srinivas wrote an ethnographic study of beauty parlours?

elements.title: New Orleans Marine Hospital
elements.contents: ... that the first New Orleans Marine Hospital exploded, the second sank partially into a swamp, and the third was given a jazz funeral?
